description_long: This is a small wooden picket fence section, featuring seven vertical slats with pointed tops, two of which are stained a darker brown while the rest are painted a light gray. The fence is supported by two horizontal rails, also painted gray, and is accompanied by a paint can and brush, suggesting ongoing maintenance or painting. The brush is resting on one of the slats, with visible paint marks on the brown slats, indicating a transition from brown to gray. The slats are evenly spaced and the overall construction appears sturdy, typical of residential or garden fencing. The paint can and brush are placed in front of the fence, adding a sense of realism and context. The design is simple and functional, with no ornate or decorative elements beyond the pointed tops of the slats.
description: A small wooden picket fence with pointed tops, featuring both brown and gray slats, accompanied by a paint can and brush. The fence is sturdy and simple, with visible signs of painting in progress.
description_view_0: Front view showing all seven slats, two brown and five gray, with a paint can and brush in front and visible paint marks on the slats.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the fence and the protruding paintbrush resting on a slat, with the paint can visible beside it.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the slats and rails, with the brown and gray color contrast still visible but no paint can or brush in sight.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the paintbrush handle and the paint can, emphasizing the depth and construction of the fence.
